project.name: vim-rogue
project.description: A roguelike dungeon game that teaches Vim motions through gameplay. Players navigate four 80×40 dungeon levels using real Vim keybindings (hjkl, wb, G/gg, 0$, ft, dd), dodge FOV-aware enemies, and manage lives/HP while progressing through zones that gate new motion mechanics.

nodes.name: Game Lifecycle
nodes.summary: Manages the overall game state transitions from launch through gameplay to win/loss conditions. Handles initialization, pause/resume, level progression, and session persistence.

businessRules: Game starts in Title state, transitions to Playing on first input
Three pause options: Resume, Restart Level, Quit Game
Won state when completing Level 4
Lost state when lives reach 0
Death with checkpoint: respawn with full HP at checkpoint
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers Level Generation when advancing levels
Resets Player Movement state on retry
Disables Enemy AI during pause/menu states

nodes.name: Player Movement
nodes.summary: Core gameplay mechanic teaching Vim motions through dungeon navigation. Thirteen motion types gated across five zones, with boundary checking, wall collision, and motion discovery tracking.

businessRules: Zone 1 (gray): hjkl basic movements
Zone 2 (cyan): wb horizontal scan (stop at non-passable)
Zone 3 (magenta): 0$ line boundaries, G/gg vertical scan
Zone 4 (red): ft character search (requires both keys)
Zone 5 (gold): dd destroys obstacles only
All motions check map boundaries and wall collisions
Motion discovery tracked per session
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers Collision Detection on enemy contact
Updates Visibility/FOV after each motion
Consumes Input State pending key buffer for f/t/dd/gg

nodes.name: Enemy AI
nodes.summary: FOV-aware enemy behavior combining BFS pathfinding chase logic with room-based patrol. Enemies pursue player when visible, patrol designated areas otherwise.

businessRules: FOV radius 8 for enemy vision (has_line_of_sight via Bresenham)
Player visible: BFS shortest path chase (step_toward_player)
Player not visible: patrol within PatrolArea boundaries
Level 4 enemies have hp: Some(30), require 3 melee hits
All enemies deal 10 damage on collision
Enemy turn executes after player motion completes
crossDomainInteractions: Queries Visibility system for line-of-sight
Triggers Combat System on player collision
Animates movement using Animation timing

nodes.name: Level Generation
nodes.summary: Procedural dungeon creation across four distinct levels with increasing complexity. Carves corridors, places walls/obstacles, spawns enemies, defines patrol areas, and positions torchlight checkpoints.

businessRules: 80×40 grid, row-major indexing (grid[y][x])
Level 1: basic layout with 5 zones
Level 2: inverted maze + obstacles (dd destroys)
Level 3: zigzag corridors + BFS enemies
Level 4: fortress rooms + FOV patrol + HP enemies
Zone colors: gray→cyan→magenta→red→gold
Torchlight provides permanent FOV radius 6 when activated
Exit positioned in Zone 5 (gold zone)
crossDomainInteractions: Populates Enemy AI with spawn positions and patrol areas
Initializes Visibility system with tile structure
Sets Player Movement boundary constraints

nodes.name: Combat System
nodes.summary: Handles damage calculation, life deduction, enemy HP, and melee combat through the x key. Manages death, respawn, and checkpoint recovery mechanics.

businessRules: MAX_HP = 30, enemy collision = 10 damage
Lives: 3 total, enemy collision = -1 life
0 lives → Lost state (game over)
Melee (x key): 10 damage to adjacent enemies
Level 4 enemies have 30 HP (3 melee hits to kill)
Death without checkpoint: retry level from start
Death with checkpoint: respawn at checkpoint with full HP
crossDomainInteractions: Triggered by Enemy AI collision events
Resets Player Movement position on respawn
Updates Game Lifecycle to Lost state when lives = 0

nodes.name: Visibility System
nodes.summary: Implements fog-of-war through field-of-view computation using ray-casting. Manages three tile states (Hidden, Explored, Visible) with permanent exploration tracking.

businessRules: FOV_RADIUS: default player vision radius
Torchlight: permanent FOV radius 6 when activated
Hidden: never seen, black
Visible: currently in FOV, full brightness
Explored: seen before but not currently visible, dim gray
Explored tiles persist permanently (memory)
crossDomainInteractions: Queried by Enemy AI for line-of-sight checks
Updated after Player Movement
Rendered by Presentation system (fog-of-war effect)

nodes.name: Input Handling
nodes.summary: Processes keyboard input through bracket-lib event loop. Manages pending state for two-key motions (f, t, dd, gg) and maps raw keys to VimMotion enum variants.

businessRules: ESC/q: pause game
f + char: find next character (requires pending state)
t + char: till before character (requires pending state)
dd: destroy obstacle (requires pending state)
gg/G: jump to top/bottom (gg requires pending state)
All other motions: single key immediate execution
crossDomainInteractions: Feeds Player Movement with parsed VimMotion
Triggers Game Lifecycle pause on ESC/q

nodes.name: Presentation
nodes.summary: Pure rendering system using bracket-lib with zero state mutation. Draws title screen, game viewport, UI sidebar, minimap, fog-of-war, and win/loss/pause overlays.

businessRules: Never mutates App state (read-only)
Zone-colored walls: gray→cyan→magenta→red→gold
Sidebar: HP bar, lives count, motion discovery list
Minimap: 80×40 condensed view
Fog-of-war: Hidden (black), Explored (dim), Visible (bright)
ASCII art for title and win/loss screens
crossDomainInteractions: Reads all other domains for display data
No state mutation (pure function)

nodes.name: Audio & Animation
nodes.summary: Polish layer providing smooth movement animations and sound effects. Uses easing functions and separate timing for player (150ms) vs enemy (200ms) movements.

businessRules: PLAYER_MOVE_MS = 150ms animation duration
ENEMY_MOVE_MS = 200ms animation duration
Interpolator provides easing functions
RealClock for production, TestClock for tests
Audio disabled by default, requires enable()
SoundEffect enum with graceful fallback
crossDomainInteractions: Polishes Player Movement with motion animations
Polishes Enemy AI with chase/patrol animations
Polishes Combat with damage/death sounds

nodes.name: Start Game
nodes.summary: Initializes the game session, displays title screen, and transitions to active gameplay on first input.

nodes.name: Play Level
nodes.summary: Core gameplay loop: player input → motion validation → collision check → enemy turn → animation update → render. Repeats until level exit or death.

nodes.name: Complete Level
nodes.summary: Player reaches exit tile → increment level index → generate new level → respawn player at start → restore visibility → continue gameplay.

nodes.name: Player Death
nodes.summary: Enemy collision → deduct 10 HP → decrement lives → check loss condition → respawn (with checkpoint if active) or game over.

nodes.name: Melee Attack
nodes.summary: Player presses x → check adjacent enemies → deal 10 damage → remove dead enemies (HP ≤ 0) → play animation/sound.

nodes.name: Pause Game
nodes.summary: Player presses ESC/q → transition to Paused state → render pause overlay → await resume/restart/quit selection.

nodes.name: Enemy Turn
nodes.summary: After player motion → check visibility for each enemy → chase (BFS) or patrol → move → check collision with player → deal damage if contact.

nodes.name: Two-Key Motion
nodes.summary: First key (f/t/d/g) → store in pending state → await second key → parse complete motion → execute → clear pending state.

domainMeta.entryPoint: f, t, d, or g key press

nodes.name: Activate Checkpoint
nodes.summary: Player touches torchlight → set permanent FOV radius 6 → store checkpoint position → enable respawn at this location on death.

nodes.name: Initialize App
nodes.summary: Create App instance with initial game state (Title), Level 1 map, player at spawn position, default InputState.

nodes.name: Render Title Screen
nodes.summary: Display ASCII art title, controls instructions, and 'press any key' prompt using bracket-lib console.

nodes.name: Wait For First Input
nodes.summary: Block in event loop until any key press, then transition from GameState::Title to GameState::Playing.

nodes.name: Generate Level 1
nodes.summary: Create 80×40 map with 5 zones (gray→cyan→magenta→red→gold), basic corridors, enemy spawns, patrol areas, torchlight, exit.

nodes.name: Parse Key Input
nodes.summary: Read bracket-lib key event, check for pause (ESC/q), check pending state for two-key motions, map to VimMotion enum.

nodes.name: Validate Motion Boundaries
nodes.summary: Check if target position is within 80×40 grid bounds, reject if out of bounds.

nodes.name: Check Wall Collision
nodes.summary: Check if target tile is passable (Floor, Exit, Torchlight), stop at walls/obstacles unless dd motion.

nodes.name: Execute Player Motion
nodes.summary: Update player position, add to motion trail, increment motion count, mark motion as discovered, trigger animation.

nodes.name: Update Visibility
nodes.summary: Recompute FOV from new player position using ray-casting, mark newly visible tiles as explored.

nodes.name: Check Enemy Visibility
nodes.summary: For each enemy, use Bresenham line algorithm to check if player is within FOV radius 8 (has_line_of_sight).

nodes.name: BFS Chase Player
nodes.summary: If player visible, compute shortest path via BFS, move one step toward player (step_toward_player).

nodes.name: Patrol Area
nodes.summary: If player not visible, move within PatrolArea boundaries using predefined patrol pattern.

nodes.name: Check Player Collision
nodes.summary: After enemy move, check if enemy position == player position, trigger damage if true.

nodes.name: Deduct HP
nodes.summary: Subtract 10 HP from player on enemy collision, play damage sound, trigger damage animation.

nodes.name: Decrement Lives
nodes.summary: Lives -= 1 on enemy collision, play death sound, check for game over condition.

nodes.name: Check Loss Condition
nodes.summary: If lives == 0, transition to GameState::Lost, display game over screen, await restart.

nodes.name: Respawn Player
nodes.summary: If checkpoint active, teleport player to checkpoint position; otherwise, reset to level start. Restore full HP.

nodes.name: Check Exit Reach
nodes.summary: After player motion, check if player position == exit tile position, trigger level completion if true.

nodes.name: Increment Level
nodes.summary: Level += 1, check for win condition (Level > 4), otherwise generate next level layout.

nodes.name: Generate Next Level
nodes.summary: Procedurally generate level 2/3/4 with appropriate layout (maze/zigzag/fortress), enemy spawns, patrol areas.

nodes.name: Check Win Condition
nodes.summary: If Level > 4, transition to GameState::Won, display victory screen, await restart.

nodes.name: Melee Damage Enemy
nodes.summary: Subtract 10 HP from adjacent enemy, play attack sound, remove enemy if HP ≤ 0.

nodes.name: Transition Paused
nodes.summary: Set GameState::Paused, render pause overlay with options (Resume, Restart, Quit).

nodes.name: Handle Pause Selection
nodes.summary: Process user choice: Resume (restore Playing), Restart Level (reset to level start), Quit (exit game).

nodes.name: Store Pending Key
nodes.summary: On f/t/d/g press, store key in InputState.pending_motion, await second key.

nodes.name: Parse Two-Key Combo
nodes.summary: On second key press, combine pending_motion + current key into VimMotion (f+char, t+char, dd, gg).

nodes.name: Clear Pending State
nodes.summary: After two-key motion execution or invalid combo, reset InputState.pending_motion to None.

nodes.name: Set Checkpoint
nodes.summary: Store torchlight position in Session.checkpoint, set Session.has_checkpoint = true, activate permanent FOV.

nodes.name: Activate Permanent FOV
nodes.summary: Set VisibilityMap permanent_radius = 6, torchlight tiles always provide FOV regardless of player position.

nodes.name: Render Game Viewport
nodes.summary: Draw visible tiles with zone-colored walls, player @ symbol, enemies (E), fog-of-war overlay, torchlight.

nodes.name: Render UI Sidebar
nodes.summary: Draw HP bar, lives count, level number, motion discovery list, zone progression indicators.

nodes.name: Render Minimap
nodes.summary: Draw condensed 80×40 map overview with explored areas, player position, exit marker.

nodes.name: Update Animations
nodes.summary: Tick AnimationTimer, update interpolation values for active animations, mark completed animations for cleanup.

nodes.name: Play Sound Effect
nodes.summary: If audio enabled, play SoundEffect (move/damage/death/melee), graceful fallback if disabled.

nodes.name: Scan Horizontal (w/b)
nodes.summary: w: scan forward until non-passable, b: scan backward until non-passable. Stop at walls/obstacles.

nodes.name: Scan Vertical (G/gg)
nodes.summary: G: jump to bottom of map, gg: jump to top of map. Stop at first non-passable tile.

nodes.name: Search Character (f/t)
nodes.summary: f: find next occurrence of character in current line, t: till before character. Scan forward only.

nodes.name: Destroy Obstacle (dd)
nodes.summary: dd: if target tile is Tile::Obstacle, change to Tile::Floor (destruction). No effect on other tiles.

nodes.name: Track Motion Discovery
nodes.summary: On first use of each VimMotion, add to PlayerState.discovered_motions set, persist for session.

nodes.name: Update Motion Trail
nodes.summary: Add old position to PlayerState.motion_trail, limit trail length, use for rendering player path.

edges.description: Player motion triggers enemy collision detection
Player motions constrained by map boundaries and tile types
Enemy AI queries line-of-sight for chase decisions
Enemy movement triggers player collision and damage
Map generation places enemies in patrol areas and spawn points
Respawn resets player position (with or without checkpoint)
Map structure defines passable tiles and boundaries for motions
Map generation defines patrol areas for enemy behavior
Map tile types determine FOV ray-casting behavior
Parsed key inputs execute VimMotion on player state
Polishes player motions with smooth animations
Polishes enemy movements with animations and sounds
Polishes combat events with damage/death sounds
Level completion triggers procedural generation of next level
